summary: Initial test for Habit Cross Daily Streaker. Backend: all 5 API tests pass (root, /api/assets, /api/assets/keys with 8 correct keys, /api/assets/generate cached & default-body). 8 PNG assets generated by Gemini Nano Banana are present and valid (~6.07 MB total base64). Frontend: full onboarding -> tabs -> add habit -> toggle done -> detail -> stats -> settings -> reset flow works end-to-end. Two real findings: (1) tabBarTestID values tab-road/tab-stats/tab-settings are NOT rendered as data-testid attributes by expo-router web (tab clicks succeed via role/text, but selectors specified in the spec do not exist in DOM); (2) AssetPack (~6 MB) overflows AsyncStorage on web (QuotaExceededError) and is dangerously close to RN AsyncStorage Android default cap (6 MB) — should be persisted to FileSystem instead. Several React Native deprecation warnings detected (shadow*, Image.style.resizeMode, props.pointerEvents).

ui_bugs.issue: tabBarTestID 'tab-road' / 'tab-stats' / 'tab-settings' are not applied to the rendered tab buttons on the Expo web preview. DOM tabs (role='tab') have testid=null. Spec requires these testIDs. Tabs themselves work and can be clicked via role/aria-label/text.

integration_issues.issue: [storage] setItem(assets.pack.v1) failed QuotaExceededError on web (>5 MB localStorage). Pack is ~6.07 MB of base64 PNGs (largest tile 893 KB). On Android, AsyncStorage default ceiling is 6 MB — pack is right at the limit and is very likely to fail in production on devices. Recommendation: persist each PNG to expo-file-system (writeAsStringAsync, base64 encoding) and store only file URIs in AsyncStorage. The app still functions because it falls back to the network/in-memory pack, but offline caching is effectively broken on web preview.

issues: Deprecation warning: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use 'boxShadow' (multiple components, e.g. FAB on (tabs)/index.tsx).
Deprecation warning: Image style.resizeMode is deprecated. Move to props.resizeMode — onboarding.tsx illustration style and likely other Image components.
Deprecation warning: props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents (RN internal Animated.View usage).

react_native_issues.issue: tabBarTestID does not render as data-testid on web build of expo-router; spec testIDs tab-road/tab-stats/tab-settings missing. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx:28,38,48
Uses style.resizeMode='contain' instead of props.resizeMode='contain'. Reference: /app/frontend/app/onboarding.tsx:172
shadow*-family props are deprecated in React Native (and trigger console warnings on web). Replace with boxShadow / elevation. Example: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:236-239

action_items: Expose tab-bar testIDs in DOM: tabBarTestID on expo-router web does not propagate as data-testid. Wrap the tabBarIcon with a <View testID='tab-road'>… (per Tabs.Screen) OR use a custom tabBar/tabBarButton that forwards testID so [data-testid="tab-road|tab-stats|tab-settings"] work in tests.

Persist asset PNGs to expo-file-system (e.g. <documentDirectory>/assets/<key>.png) and only keep URIs in AsyncStorage. Current ~6 MB pack overflows web localStorage and risks Android AsyncStorage 6 MB cap. The QuotaExceededError is visible in console on every load.
Move Image resizeMode from style to props (onboarding.tsx illustration, plus any other Image components using style.resizeMode).
Replace shadow* style props with boxShadow / elevation (FAB and summary cards in (tabs)/index.tsx, settings cards, etc.) to silence deprecation warnings and stay future-proof.
Consider downsizing Gemini PNGs (target <100 KB each, e.g. 256x256) — every asset is currently 600-900 KB base64 which is heavy for a mobile asset pack that has to be cached locally.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py uses os.environ['MONGO_URL'] and os.environ['DB_NAME'] correctly; CORS open '*' is fine for this offline-first design.
Asset generate endpoint returns full base64 payload (>6 MB) per call — fine for a single warm-up but you may want a streaming or per-key fetch for OTA updates later.
Frontend properly uses process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL; no hardcoded URLs spotted.
All interactive elements (habit-card-*, done-today-*, detail-*, cal-*, onboarding-*, settings) have correct testIDs except for the tab bar.
seed_data_creation: None. Habits were created/deleted within the test via UI and cleaned up by the in-app 'Reset all data' flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend assets are already cached in MongoDB; /api/assets returns ready=true with 8 keys. To exercise onboarding on web, run page.evaluate to localStorage.clear() before each test (the app persists onboardingDone in AsyncStorage backed by localStorage). To click tab buttons on web preview use page.get_by_role('tab', name='Stats|Settings|Road') — tabBarTestID currently does not become data-testid. Watch for console warning '[storage] setItem(assets.pack.v1) failed QuotaExceededError' — that is the AsyncStorage cap issue.
rca_of_issue: Tab testIDs: expo-router's web Tabs renders the tab bar via react-navigation/bottom-tabs whose web implementation does not forward tabBarTestID to the underlying button (known limitation). Either upgrade the bottom-tabs lib that supports the prop on web, supply a custom tabBarButton that injects testID via accessibilityState, or wrap tabBarIcon in a View with testID. AsyncStorage quota: the assets pack JSON.stringify result is ~6.07 MB which exceeds browser localStorage (~5 MB) and is right at the Android AsyncStorage default ceiling (6 MB). Fix by writing PNGs to expo-file-system and only persisting file paths.
